subject: Biology
title: Translation: Building Proteins
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Translation: Building Proteins.

hook.text: Ribosomes read mRNA three letters at a time. Each triplet, called a codon, codes for one amino acid.

buildup.text: Transfer RNA molecules bring the right amino acid to match each codon on the mRNA.

discovery.text: Amino acids link together in a chain that folds into a functional protein.

twist.text: A single mRNA can be read by many ribosomes at once, producing proteins in bulk.

climax.text: From insulin to collagen, every protein starts as a string of codons on mRNA.

punchline.text: Three letters at a time, proteins are born.

quiz.question: What is a codon?
options: A three-letter sequence coding for an amino acid
